content.text: I'm not able to give Sol a specific ml dose — dosing for infant acetaminophen is calculated by **weight**, not age, and I don't have Sol's current weight on file. Getting the dose wrong in either direction matters here, so this one really needs to go straight to your pediatric office or a nurse line (most clinics have an after-hours line for exactly this). They can give you the right ml amount in under a minute once you tell them Sol's weight. If it's after hours and you can't reach anyone, a pharmacist can also calculate a weight-based dose over the phone. What I'd hold off on is guessing from an age chart on the box — those are conservative estimates and may not be right for Sol's size. Call first, dose second.
